diagnostics.1: So close! You're off by just 1. You're 1 short — try counting one more time.
diagnostics.2: Watch out for off-by-one slips here.

hint.level_1: Try with simpler numbers first, then scale up.
hint.level_2: Can you break it into smaller steps?
hint.level_3: Try with simpler numbers first, then scale up.
hint.level_4: Write down what you have on each side.
hint.level_5: Skip-counting tip: keep a tally as you go.

questions.visual_context: A number line with skip-counting jumps marked.
solution_steps: Look at what you need to count
Count systematically one by one
The total is your answer

questions.stem: Help Ranger Roo figure out the count: It is 5:45. What time will it be in 15 minutes?

diagnostics.0: 6:15 is too far — you added 30 minutes, not 15.
diagnostics.1: 7:00 is a whole hour ahead — only 15 minutes pass.
diagnostics.3: 5:00 is earlier — time moves forward, not backward.

hint.level_0: Look at the minutes on the clock.
hint.level_1: Identify which operation to use.
hint.level_3: 60 minutes = 1 hour. The hour hand moves up.
hint.level_4: 5 o'clock + 1 hour = 6 o'clock.

questions.visual_context: An analog clock face showing the time from the problem.
solution_steps: Look at what you need to count
Count systematically one by one
